body:
## Intent-based tool-call routing in `formal-ai serve` (fixes #680)

Tool-call emission was **phrasing-gated rather than intent-based**: the deterministic
planner only emitted a `web_search` / `web_fetch` / `write` / `edit` / shell call when the
request matched a narrow, hardcoded phrasing (or the pinned formalization recipe). A
request that *meant* the same thing in different words fell through to a prose
description. The issue's live baseline over 50 prompts each:

| capability | before | after |
|---|---|---|
| web_search | 0/50 | routes on intent |
| web_fetch  | 0/50 | routes on intent |
| edit       | 0/50 | routes on intent |
| write      | 4/50 | routes on intent |
| shell      | 6/50 | routes on intent |

This PR makes emission a function of **intent** — the advertised tool set plus request
semantics — across all five capabilities and all three wire surfaces the target CLIs use
(OpenAI Chat Completions, OpenAI Responses, Gemini `generateContent`), for codex,
opencode, gemini, qwen, and `@link-assistant/agent`.

### How it routes

A request routes to a real `tool_call` when it expresses a capability's intent **and**
the matching capability tool is actually advertised; otherwise it still falls through to
the prose answer (a client can never be handed a call it cannot honour). Each capability
is recognised from the **seed lexicon**, never from hardcoded natural language in the
planner (CONTRIBUTING §2) — a maintainer retunes coverage by editing a `.lino` file:

- **web_search / web_fetch** — an implicit research question, a source lookup, or a URL
  fetch routes to the advertised search/fetch tool with a non-empty `query` / the exact
  `url`, in any phrasing across en/ru/hi/zh.
- **write** — a file-creation intent naming a relative target and literal content routes
  to the write tool. Recognised via the new `file_write_*` roles
  (`data/seed/meanings-file-write.lino`) and probed *before* the file-read router so
  "create file X containing Y" is a write, not a read of X.
- **edit** — a file-modification intent naming a target plus an old→new replacement
  routes to whatever the CLI calls its edit tool (`edit`, `replace`, `apply_patch`,
  `str_replace`). The new `Capability::Edit` recovers the `(target, old, new)` triple from
  the `file_edit_*` roles (`data/seed/meanings-file-edit.lino`) and emits every common
  argument-key alias so one plan drives any CLI's edit tool.
- **shell** — a semantic shell request that never names the command ("Print the current
  working directory" → `pwd`, "How much disk space is free?" → `df -h`, "What is my
  username?" → `whoami`) routes to the run tool with the concrete command. The
  intent→command table, multilingual cue phrases (en/ru/hi/zh), and per-intent argument
  recovery (`wc -l Cargo.toml`, `mkdir build`) live in the new
  `data/seed/shell-intents.lino`, running as a fallback after the named-command (#676)
  and directory-listing routers so existing shell behaviour is unchanged.

### Architecture

- `Capability` enum gains `Edit`; `plan_chat_step` routes each capability through the same
  data-driven `tool_for(tool_names, capability)` classifier — no per-phrasing branches.
- The issue-680 capability probes (`plan_web_search_step`, `plan_web_fetch_step`,
  `plan_edit_step` and their helpers) live in a new sibling module
  `src/agentic_coding/intent_router.rs`, sharing the planner's now-`pub(super)` step
  primitives (`Progress`, `tool_for`, `plan_one`, `fetch_arguments`). This keeps
  `planner.rs` under the repo's 1000-line hard limit; behaviour is unchanged.
- `shell_command_for_task` gains a third, seed-backed fallback strategy
  (`intent_shell_command`) alongside the existing #676 named-command and directory-listing
  strategies.
- Natural language stays out of the engine: intents, replacement leads, argument tags, and
  cue words are all quoted scalar data in `data/seed/*.lino`, so the **total-closure audit
  stays at zero** unresolved tokens.
- The committed self-census fixtures (`data/meta/self-ast.lino`,
  `data/meta/self-healing-case.lino`, `docs/case-studies/issue-538/…self-ast.json`) are
  regenerated from the real drivers so they match the smaller `planner.rs` byte-for-byte.

### Reproduction & tests

Every capability has a reproducing test that fails on `main` and passes here, each row
using a **different** phrasing/language so a pass proves the routing is general, not
memorised (CONTRIBUTING rule 4):

- `tests/unit/issue_680_intent_routing.rs` — per-capability routing, the eight issue shell
  examples (`ls`, `pwd`, `date`, `df -h`, `ps`, `wc -l Cargo.toml`, `mkdir build`,
  `whoami`) plus Russian/Hindi/Chinese shell rows proving the routing is language-agnostic
  (en/ru/hi/zh), negative cases (no matching tool → no fabricated call; read not stolen by
  the write router; write not stolen by the edit router), and second-step completion.
- `tests/integration/issue_680_intent_routing.rs` — the same intents end-to-end over Chat
  Completions, Responses, and Gemini `generateContent`.

Local gates green after merging `main`: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets
--all-features`, `scripts/sync-seed.sh --check`, the language test-coverage gate
(en/ru/hi/zh), the file-size limit, the total-closure audit (0 unresolved), and the full
`cargo test` suite.

Fixes #680

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/680

Collect data related to issue #680 into
dev/log/issues/680/pulls/683/:
- issue/   full issue #680 (json+md) and comments
- pull/    PR #683 (json+md+diff), conversation/review comments, reviews
- related/ referenced issues #681 #607 #602 #604 #628 #671
- code/    snapshots of the shared planner/solver-handler source that is
           the root cause, plus code-references.md mapping symptoms to lines
- README.md index summarizing the issue matrix and folder layout

Remove the bootstrap .gitkeep now that real content exists.
commits.messageHeadline: docs(issue-680): compile issue/PR/related data into dev/log
Add development log for issue #680 PR #683

commits.messageBody:
The deterministic planner never emitted a web_search or web_fetch tool
call for a general request — those capabilities only fired inside pinned
formalization recipes, so tool_calls were phrasing-gated rather than
intent-based. Expose the existing lexicon-driven detectors as public
probes (http_fetch_url_for / web_search_query_for) and add a general
capability router to plan_chat_step that emits the matching tool call
when the request carries the intent AND the client advertised a tool of
that capability (otherwise it falls through to the prose answer).

Probes run most-specific-first (fetch -> write -> search) so a CLI that
advertises every tool at once still routes each request correctly. Adds
'download' fetch cues to the http_fetch seed meaning across en/ru/hi/zh.

Tests: unit (issue_680_intent_routing) proves intent routing in varied
phrasings/languages; integration boots a real agent-mode server and
asserts tool_calls emit across all three wire surfaces (Chat
Completions, Responses, Gemini generateContent).
commits.messageHeadline: fix(agentic): route web-search & web-fetch intents to tool calls (#680)

commits.messageBody:
… census (#680)

- Probe the general write router before the file-read router so a file-creation
  request ("create file X containing Y") routes to the advertised write tool
  instead of being mistaken for a read of X.
- Recognise the write intent (target/content/action/destination) entirely from
  the seed lexicon via new file_write_* roles (data/seed/meanings-file-write.lino),
  keeping natural language as data (CONTRIBUTING §2).
- clean_path_token trims trailing sentence punctuation so a word that merely ends
  a sentence is not mistaken for a dotted filename.
- "загрузи" moves from http_fetch to url_navigate; "скачай" stays the fetch verb.
- Regenerate the three census-embedding fixtures (self-ast.lino,
  agent-cli-session-self-ast.json, self-healing-case.lino) for the new planner AST.
- Add unit + integration intent-routing tests across Chat Completions, Responses,
  and Gemini surfaces; changelog fragment.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(agentic): route write intent before file-read; regen self-healing…

commits.messageBody:
Add Capability::Edit so a file-modification request ("In greeting.txt,
change hello to goodbye", "Replace foo with bar in notes.txt", Russian
"замени … на … в файле …") routes to whatever edit tool a client
advertises (edit/replace/apply_patch/str_replace) instead of returning a
prose description.

- compose_edit_request recovers the (target, old, new) triple entirely
  from the seed lexicon via the new file_edit_* roles
  (data/seed/meanings-file-edit.lino), detecting the target cue on either
  side of the file token and walking the clause boundary so multi-word
  cues ("в файле") and SOV order do not leak into the new text.
- classify_tool maps edit/patch/replace to Capability::Edit; plan_edit_step
  emits every common argument-key alias (path/filePath/file_path,
  oldString/old_string/old_str/old, newString/new_string/new_str/new) so
  one plan drives any CLI's edit tool. Probed after the create-file write
  router and before the file-read router.
- Grant tool:capability:edit in default_associative_packages so the
  permission policy admits edit tools.
- Fix: add MEANINGS_FILE_EDIT_LINO to the MEANING_FILES array so the
  file_edit_* roles actually load into the lexicon.
- Regenerate self-AST census fixtures (22972 links / 4666 named nodes).

Tests: 11 unit + 8 integration issue_680 routing cases (en/ru), all
three wire surfaces; census tests green.
commits.messageHeadline: feat(agentic): route file-modification intent to edit tool (#680)

commits.messageBody:
A shell request that never names the command — expressing an *intent*
such as "Print the current working directory", "How much disk space is
free?", or "What is my username?" — now routes to the advertised run
tool carrying the concrete command (pwd, df -h, whoami) instead of a
prose answer.

The intent->command table lives entirely in the new
data/seed/shell-intents.lino: multilingual cue phrases (en/ru/hi/zh),
the concrete command, and a per-intent argument kind (none/path/
name_lead) with the name-lead cue words used to recover a name. A new
ShellIntentVocabulary loader (src/seed/shell_intents.rs) parses it, and
shell_command_for_task gains a third fallback strategy
(intent_shell_command) that fires only after the named-command (#676)
and directory-listing routers, so existing shell behaviour is
unchanged. Arguments are recovered generally: a filename-looking token
for `wc -l Cargo.toml`, and the token after a name-lead word for
`mkdir build`; an intent whose required argument is absent is skipped
so the search continues rather than emitting a command that would hang.

Natural language stays out of the planner (CONTRIBUTING §2): the enum
tags and name-lead surface words are quoted scalar data in the seed
file, so the total-closure audit treats them as literal values, and
coverage is retuned by editing seed data rather than Rust.

Unit and integration tests assert all eight issue examples route to the
run tool with the exact command across three wire surfaces, that no
shell call is fabricated when no run tool is advertised, and the
total-closure gate stays at zero.
commits.messageHeadline: feat(agentic): route semantic shell intents to the run tool (issue #680)

commits.messageBody:
…under size limit

The issue-#680 edit/write/web routing pushed src/agentic_coding/planner.rs
to 1100 lines, over the repo's 1000-line hard limit for Rust files
(CI "Lint and Format Check" → "Check file size limit" failed with exit 1).

Extract the three general, intent-based capability probes and their
helpers into a new sibling module src/agentic_coding/intent_router.rs:
plan_web_fetch_step, plan_web_search_step, plan_edit_step, the two
*_final_answer summaries, and edit_arguments. The probes share the
planner's own step primitives, which are now pub(super): Progress (with
new fetched_text()/search_output() accessors), tool_for, plan_one, and
fetch_arguments. plan_chat_step calls them through intent_router::* so
behaviour is unchanged. planner.rs drops to 989 lines.

Because self_ast.rs computes its CST/AST census from planner.rs via
include_str!, shrinking the module drifts the committed census, so the
two byte-for-byte fixtures are regenerated from the new source:
data/meta/self-ast.lino and
docs/case-studies/issue-538/agent-cli-session-self-ast.json (node counts
fall consistently with the smaller module).

All gates green locally: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets
--all-features, scripts/sync-seed.sh --check, the total-closure audit
(0 unresolved), the issue_538 census suite (32 tests, fixtures match
byte-for-byte), and the issue_680 intent-routing suite (13 tests).
commits.messageHeadline: refactor(agentic): extract issue-680 capability router; keep planner …

commits.messageBody:
The 'Check language test coverage' CI gate requires the PR's added test
lines to collectively cover every supported language (en, ru, hi, zh)
whenever language-facing seed/handler files change. The shell intent
router draws its cues from data/seed/shell-intents.lino, which spans all
four languages, so extend shell_intent_routes_to_run_tool_in_any_phrasing
with genuine cue-matching rows in Russian, Hindi and Chinese (and label
each row's language), proving the routing recovers the same concrete
command regardless of language (CONTRIBUTING rule 4).

Verified: cd tests/e2e && GITHUB_BASE_REF=main node
scripts/check-language-test-coverage.mjs reports OK for en, ru, hi, zh;
cargo test --test unit -- issue_680 (13 passed).
commits.messageHeadline: test(issue-680): cover en/ru/hi/zh in shell intent routing

commits.messageBody:
…xtraction

Extracting the issue-680 router cluster out of src/agentic_coding/planner.rs
shrank the module, changing its CST/AST census (total_link_count
22972->20648, named_node_count 4666->4161). Three committed fixtures embed
that census; the earlier commit regenerated data/meta/self-ast.lino and the
issue-538 session JSON but missed the self-healing repair case, which pins
the same source_round_trip counts. Regenerate it from the real self-healing
loop via 'cargo run --example dump_self_healing_case' so
committed_self_healing_case_is_generated_and_written_by_the_driver
(tests/unit/issue_558_self_healing.rs:172) matches byte-for-byte again.

Verified: cargo test --test unit -- issue_558_self_healing::committed
issue_538 (33 passed).
commits.messageHeadline: chore(issue-680): regenerate self-healing-case census after planner e…

title: feat: intent-based tool-call routing in `formal-ai serve` (web_search, web_fetch, write, edit, shell)
